description:
You are the system administrator for a small fleet of Linux-based servers.  
    On disk you already have the following two files:

      1. /home/user/server_data/inventory.json  
      2. /home/user/server_data/inventory_schema.json  

    Your job is to:

    1) Validate that the JSON inventory (/home/user/server_data/inventory.json) conforms to the
       JSON-Schema stored in /home/user/server_data/inventory_schema.json.
       • If the file is valid, create the file /home/user/server_data/validation.log
         containing exactly one line:
             SCHEMA_VALID
         (followed by a single trailing newline).
       • If the file is NOT valid, the same log file must contain exactly one line that
         begins with the word SCHEMA_INVALID, a single space, and then the first validation
         error message returned by the validator.  End the line with a newline.
       • Nothing else (no timestamps or extra whitespace) should be written to
         /home/user/server_data/validation.log.

    2) Using jq, produce a list of servers that still run a kernel whose **major**
       version is lower than 5 (e.g. 4.19, 3.10, etc.).
       • Read the kernel string from each element in the “servers” array of
         inventory.json, split on the first “.”, turn the substring before the dot
         into an integer, and compare it to 5.
       • For every server that meets the “older than 5” condition, write its hostname
         to /home/user/server_data/outdated_kernels.log.
       • The log must contain one hostname per line, sorted alphabetically, and end
         with a single trailing newline.  No other text is allowed in the file.

    Notes & constraints:
      • Do not move or rename any of the existing JSON files.
      • You can install/use any CLI tools that are available in the container
        (e.g. jq, python -m jsonschema, etc.), but the end result must be exactly
        the two log files in the required format.
      • The whole task can be completed with a small sequence of shell commands
        (≈3–4 commands is typical, but no strict limit is imposed).

    When you finish, the two log files must exist and match the exact formats
    described above so that an automated test suite can check them byte-for-byte.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated grader.
        The data below already exists in the container before the
        agent starts work.  The agent must NOT see this section.
    -->

    <!-- Initial files on disk -->
    <file path="/home/user/server_data/inventory.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "servers": [
    {
      "hostname": "web-01",
      "ip": "10.0.0.11",
      "kernel": "5.15.0-60-generic",
      "os_release": "Ubuntu 22.04"
    },
    {
      "hostname": "db-01",
      "ip": "10.0.0.21",
      "kernel": "4.19.0-17-amd64",
      "os_release": "Debian 10"
    },
    {
      "hostname": "cache-01",
      "ip": "10.0.0.31",
      "kernel": "5.10.0-18-amd64",
      "os_release": "Debian 11"
    },
    {
      "hostname": "legacy-01",
      "ip": "10.0.0.41",
      "kernel": "3.10.0-1160.el7.x86_64",
      "os_release": "CentOS 7"
    }
  ]
}
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/server_data/inventory_schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "title": "Server Inventory",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["servers"],
  "properties": {
    "servers": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": ["hostname", "ip", "kernel", "os_release"],
        "properties": {
          "hostname": { "type": "string" },
          "ip": {
            "type": "string",
            "pattern": "^(?:[0-9]{1,3}\\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$"
          },
          "kernel": { "type": "string" },
          "os_release": { "type": "string" }
        },
        "additionalProperties": false
      }
    }
  },
  "additionalProperties": false
}
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected output files after the agent finishes -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/server_data/validation.log"><![CDATA[
SCHEMA_VALID
]]></expected_file>

    <expected_file path="/home/user/server_data/outdated_kernels.log"><![CDATA[
db-01
legacy-01
]]></expected_file>
